signals.title: USD/GBP — G8 Bilateral Theme + Hawkish Fed Repricing
signals.text: USD outperforms the G8 complex by +1.097% as hawkish Fed repricing drives a broad breakout, while GBP lags at -0.986%. Despite a neutral 0bp OIS carry differential (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.73%), the USD/GBP spread of 2.083% reflects aggressive positioning into US inflation concerns and rising Treasury yields. The bear steepener regime (+59.9bp 2s10s) supports USD momentum over GBP's deteriorating relative strength. Watch for a sustained break of 1.3300 to confirm further GBP/USD downside.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% at 18.5%) contradicts the RBNZ hike bias priced for the 27 May meeting. This institutional short-bias is further challenged by an 86% retail long extreme, creating a high-conviction contrarian setup. While the +150bp USD carry advantage currently supports the pair's decline to 0.5842, a hawkish RBNZ delivery risks a violent short-squeeze against crowded speculative shorts. Watch the 0.5800 level for support or capitulation.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.70 faces increasing intervention risk as price tests the 160.00 psychological barrier. The 25d RR at -1.73 signals a strong institutional put skew, confirming the options market is paying a premium for JPY strength/USD downside protection despite the +286bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 remains crowded; intervention at 160.00 triggers a rapid unwind of carry trades. Carry dominates only while VIX remains below 25.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Positioning
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 14.3% vs HV30 at 11.1% generates a +3.2% Vol Risk Premium, the highest in the G8 space, signaling aggressive hedging against further downside. COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) shows institutional positioning remains heavily overextended just as price breaks lower to 0.7154. This combination of high vol-premium and crowded longs suggests a late-cycle trend exhaustion; a break below 0.7100 accelerates long liquidation.

signals.title: GBP/CHF — Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: GBP/CHF retail positioning reaches a 90% long extreme, diverging from the broader GBP weakness seen in the G8 scorecard (-0.986%). While the +375bp carry premium (BoE 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%) structurally favors the pair, retail's heavy long bias at 1.0484 suggests a contrarian risk of further downside if GBP sentiment continues to deteriorate. Watch for retail capitulation if price breaks the 1.0450 support level.
